summary: Carry-forward audit from ω-cycle bench-record-inner-rc-parse-fix. Required: ensure all downstream JSONL decoders/aggregators of state/kick/bench/*.jsonl recognize the new status='fail-inner-rc' (also fail-stale-witness from earlier cycle), aggregate as failure WITHOUT collapsing to plain 'fail', and preserve diagnostic distinction in detail views. Comprehensive sweep of nexus/tool/ and nexus/scripts/ for any consumer of state/kick/bench/*.jsonl returned ZERO decoders/aggregators/dashboards. Only kick_bench.hexa (producer) writes the file; kick_bench_freshness_smoke.hexa is a synthetic guard verifier (no JSONL read). All other __HEXA_RC references in atlas_absorb_sweeper / atlas_absorb_hook / kick_dispatch / witness_emit are inner-rc PROMOTERS that parse a fresh exec stream — NOT bench-JSONL consumers. Per hard policy 'honesty: if NO decoder exists yet, document HONESTLY — do NOT fabricate a decoder change', this cycle is documented as GREENFIELD: no decoder exists to update. Carry-forward becomes 'build a status-aware bench JSONL decoder/dashboard'. Synthetic JSONL with all 6 statuses written for future-decoder smoke fixture.

kernel_5op.intake: Per-record bench JSONL now emits status='fail-inner-rc' (kick_bench.hexa L537-540, post-2d40c170) and status='fail-stale-witness' (L549-550). Downstream decoders must (a) treat as failure for aggregation, (b) preserve as distinct in detail.

Diagnostic suggestion: print fix-hint string keyed on status (e.g. fail-inner-rc → 'inspect __HEXA_RC tail of last exec'; fail-stale-witness → 'witness mtime older than bench start, re-emit')
